title: caryocar brasiliense subsp. brasiliense
intro: Caryocar brasiliense subsp. brasiliense, also known as the pequi, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryocaraceae, native to the cerrado and caatinga regions of South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain various bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The seeds of the pequi plant have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and are believed to offer health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The seeds can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being consumed raw or added to traditional dishes, making it a valuable resource for local communities.
